messages.content:
Write 50 short, natural, everyday English sentences.
Write LONGER sentences, about 16 to 30 words each. Each sentence must mention at least THREE specific proper nouns from different categories — e.g. a person's name, a city or country, a company or brand, a day of the week, or a month. Vary the grammatical person across the set (first person I/we, second person you, and third person he/she/they). Vary the ending punctuation.
Output exactly one sentence per line. No numbering, no bullets, no quotation marks, no blank lines, no commentary.

content.text:
On Monday, Sarah told me that she and David were flying from London to Tokyo with Lufthansa, hoping to visit the Sony headquarters before March ended.
Did you really meet James in Paris last August, or were you thinking of that trip to Madrid when we both worked for Google?
We packed our bags on Friday because Maria insisted that driving through Berlin and Vienna in a rented Toyota would be cheaper than flying Delta.
Every December, my cousin Emily visits Chicago to see the Christmas markets, and she always buys Starbucks coffee before catching the Amtrak train home.
He swore that Michael had promised to email Amazon about the broken Samsung phone before Tuesday, but nobody in Seattle ever heard back.
You should ask Jennifer whether she prefers Netflix or Disney for the long Sunday afternoons we usually spend together at her apartment in Boston.
Last November, Robert and I drove from Toronto to Montreal in a battered Honda, listening to Spotify playlists that Anna had made for us.
They told Laura that the Microsoft office in Dublin would close early on Wednesday, so everyone planned to grab dinner near the Liffey instead.
When Carlos arrived in Barcelona on Thursday, he immediately texted Sofia to say the Zara store near the hotel was already selling summer clothes.
I can't believe you forgot that Daniel was born in Sydney, even though he kept talking about Qantas flights to Melbourne all last January!
Our teacher, Mrs. Thompson, explained that Albert Einstein once lived in Princeton, where many scientists from Germany settled before the war began in September.
She whispered to Oliver that the Apple event in San Francisco would happen on Monday, right after the big Adobe conference wrapped up downtown.
We watched Rachel and Tom argue about whether Ford or Tesla made better cars while we waited for the ferry to leave Helsinki that evening.
Did Patricia mention that her brother George moved to Amsterdam in April, joining a small startup that competes directly with Uber across northern Europe?
You and Kevin should really visit Rome this October, because Isabella says the weather there beats anything you'll find in either Oslo or Stockholm.
My grandmother always claimed that Frank Sinatra performed in Las Vegas every weekend, and she swore she once saw him there with Marilyn one Saturday.
He insisted that Nokia, Ericsson, and Siemens all sent representatives to the trade fair in Munich, though none of them stayed past Thursday afternoon.
I promised Grace that we would meet outside the Walmart in Houston on Friday, but the storm rolling in from Texas changed all our plans.
They flew with Emirates from Dubai to Singapore last June, and Nathan kept complaining that the IBM laptop he bought there overheated constantly.
Could you tell Stephanie that Henry left his Nike shoes at the gym in Manchester, and that he needs them back before Sunday morning?
We celebrated Christmas in Vienna with Lucas and Hannah, eating chocolate from Switzerland while watching old films that HBO had recommended that December.
She reminded Peter that the Coca-Cola factory near Atlanta offers tours every Wednesday, so the kids from Georgia could finally see how the syrup is made.
Honestly, you and Brian should have called Victoria before booking the Marriott in Miami, because she knows every cheap hotel along the Florida coast.
On Tuesday, Andrew told his colleagues at Intel that the new project in Bangalore would require frequent trips to both Mumbai and Delhi this year.
Did Nicole really say that Beyoncé would perform in Lisbon next May, or was she confusing that with the festival happening down in Porto?
My uncle Richard worked for Boeing in Seattle for decades, and every July he flew his family to Hawaii using the miles he had saved.
You won't believe how Christina convinced Mark to quit his job at Oracle and open a tiny café in Florence before next September arrives.
They argued all Saturday about whether Liverpool or Chelsea would win, while Joseph quietly ordered pizza from Domino's and ignored everyone in the room.
We met Samantha at the airport in Frankfurt on Monday, and she explained that her flight from Reykjavik had been delayed by Icelandair again.
He told me that Steve Jobs founded Apple in California, but Bill Gates built Microsoft up in Washington around that very same period.
Please remind Charlotte that the Ikea store in Copenhagen closes early on Sunday, so she should buy that desk before the weekend ends entirely.
Last spring, Diana and Edward toured Egypt and Jordan together, sending us photos from Cairo every Friday until their phones finally died near Petra.
I think you should ask Gabriel whether he booked the Hilton in Dubai or the one in Doha, because Aisha needs to know by Thursday.
When Megan visited New York in February, she insisted on eating at every restaurant that Anthony Bourdain had once recommended near Brooklyn Bridge.
They told Vincent that Volkswagen and BMW were both hiring engineers in Stuttgart, so he updated his résumé and emailed them the following Monday.
You really ought to thank Olivia, because she persuaded Pepsi to sponsor the marathon in Nairobi that William organized for charity last October.
We spent every Wednesday that summer learning Spanish with Diego in Seville, then practicing at little bars that Google Maps had wrongly labeled.
She confessed that Leonardo DiCaprio once filmed a movie in Cape Town, and that her sister Monica spotted him near a Shell station downtown.
Did your brother Adam ever finish that road trip from Phoenix to Denver, or did the old Chevrolet break down somewhere in Colorado again?
On Thursday, Caroline reminded everyone that Disneyland in Anaheim gets crowded during August, so booking through Expedia early usually saves a lot of money.
He swore that Cristiano Ronaldo trained in Lisbon before signing with the club, while Juventus fans in Turin still argued about it every Sunday.
We told Bradley that the FedEx package from Shanghai would arrive on Friday, but the customs delay in Hong Kong pushed everything back a week.
You should have seen how Natalie reacted when Spotify recommended that obscure band from Glasgow that she and Ryan had discovered back in March.
They insisted that Walt Disney built his first studio in Hollywood, though Pixar later transformed animation from its quiet headquarters near Oakland entirely.
My friend Lucy moved to Wellington last winter, and she keeps telling Jacob that New Zealand feels safer than either Chicago or Detroit.
Could you confirm whether Harrison booked the Air France flight to Nice on Saturday, because Eleanor wants to share a taxi from the airport?
We watched Serena Williams play in Melbourne last January, and afterward Thomas treated us to dinner at a Japanese place that Nissan executives loved.
She told her boss at HSBC that the meeting in Geneva had moved to Monday, forcing Patrick to rebook his train through Zurich entirely.
Honestly, you and Jessica should visit Lisbon in April, because Pedro promised that the festivals there rival anything you'd experience in Rio or Salvador.
On Sunday, Benjamin admitted that he had never tried McDonald's in Moscow, even though he had lived near the Kremlin throughout that whole cold winter.

messages.content:
Write 50 short, natural, everyday English sentences.
Write LONGER sentences, about 16 to 30 words each. Each sentence must mention at least THREE specific proper nouns from different categories — e.g. a person's name, a city or country, a company or brand, a day of the week, or a month. Vary the grammatical person across the set (first person I/we, second person you, and third person he/she/they). Vary the ending punctuation.
Output exactly one sentence per line. No numbering, no bullets, no quotation marks, no blank lines, no commentary.

content.text:
Last Monday, Sarah and I flew from Chicago to Toronto with Delta, hoping to finally visit the Google office before our long vacation officially started in July.

You should ask Michael whether Samsung or Apple makes the better phone before you leave London for Berlin next Friday afternoon, right?

David told us that Nike and Adidas were both opening new stores in Madrid sometime around March, which surprised everyone on Tuesday morning.

We met Jennifer at a Starbucks near Times Square in New York, where she explained how Amazon planned to expand across Texas this December.

Did you really see Emma driving a brand new Toyota through Paris last Wednesday, or was that someone else heading toward Brussels instead?

On Sunday, Robert and Lisa decided to fly Emirates from Dubai to Singapore, even though Microsoft had scheduled their big meeting for Thursday.

I honestly believe that Karen prefers Pepsi over Coca-Cola whenever she visits Atlanta, especially during those hot August weekends with her cousins.

They invited Thomas and me to watch the Lakers play in Los Angeles next October, though Facebook kept distracting everyone the entire evening.

You won't believe how James convinced Sofia to move from Barcelona to Amsterdam, just so they could both work for Spotify by January.

Every Saturday, Grandma drives her old Ford to a little market in Dublin, where Patrick sells fresh bread imported straight from Italy.

We watched Netflix together while Daniel argued that Tesla would outsell BMW across California before the chilly weather arrived in November.

Could you remind Olivia that Walmart in Houston closes early on Fridays, so she should finish shopping before meeting Brian for dinner?

He swore that Ferrari beat Mercedes during last Sunday's race in Monaco, but Andrea insisted the results changed dramatically by late September.

I packed my Sony camera before Anna and I boarded a Lufthansa flight from Munich to Tokyo early on a foggy Monday.

They keep telling Henry that Disney bought another studio in Florida, though Rachel doubts the news will actually break until February.

You really should try the pizza that Marco recommended in Rome, especially since Luigi claimed Domino's tasted nothing like it last Thursday.

We saw Chloe wearing Gucci sunglasses outside a hotel in Milan, where George said Uber drivers were striking throughout the whole April weekend.

On Wednesday, my brother Kevin downloaded WhatsApp so he could text Maria while she traveled from Lisbon to Vienna for her job.

Did Nicole actually meet Beyoncé backstage in Las Vegas, or did Steven just imagine that whole story he shared with us Friday?

I really hope you remember that Adobe and Oracle are hiring in Seattle, because Laura mentioned the deadline falls sometime this June.

They drove a rented Honda from Boston to Philadelphia, while Jacob streamed YouTube videos about Costco deals throughout the entire rainy Tuesday.

We promised Grandpa that we would visit Sydney with Qantas next May, mostly because Helen wanted to surprise Oliver for his birthday.

You should definitely warn Peter that IKEA in Stockholm gets crowded on Saturdays, especially when tourists arrive from Oslo every single summer.

She told me that Visa and Mastercard both sponsor events in Geneva, yet Frank still prefers paying cash whenever he visits Switzerland.

On Monday, Angela and I tried ordering from McDonald's in Beijing, but Zhang insisted that KFC tasted far better near the airport.

Have you heard that Cristiano signed with a club in Manchester, and that Juventus fans in Turin were absolutely heartbroken last October?

We told Brandon that HP laptops were cheaper at Best Buy in Denver, though Megan swore Dell offered better deals on Wednesdays.

I watched Julia teach Spanish in Mexico City while she used Duolingo and joked about visiting Cancun before the end of March.

Did they really cancel the concert in Glasgow because Adele caught a cold, or did Universal reschedule everything for early next December?

You and Tony should book a Hilton room in Cairo soon, since Layla mentioned that flights from Athens fill up every August.

He genuinely thinks that Intel will overtake AMD this year, although Patricia reminded him about the conference happening in Austin next Friday.

We bought groceries at Target in Phoenix before Nathan suggested watching a Cowboys game, which thrilled Diana the entire long weekend.

I told you that Greta planned to fly Ryanair from Oslo to Warsaw, but Erik decided to take the train instead Sunday.

They watched Leonardo accept his award in Hollywood, while Sophia tweeted about it and Twitter crashed across America that very night.

On Thursday, Grandma made tea for Mr. Johnson, who explained how Toyota and Mazda were competing fiercely throughout Japan this spring.

Could you tell Victoria that Zara opened a flagship store in Rome, because Antonio promised to meet her there next Monday?

We watched Messi score twice in Buenos Aires, and afterward Carlos celebrated by ordering Heineken at every bar across the whole city.

I really wish you had reminded Samuel that PayPal froze his account in Berlin, especially before he traveled to Prague last week.

She insisted that Canon cameras outperform Nikon, so Mark booked a workshop in Florence for both of them this coming September.

They flew British Airways from London to Cape Town, where Nelson showed Grace the stunning coastline early that warm February morning.

You should ask Isabella whether Honda or Subaru handles snow better, because Derek drives to Calgary almost every weekend during winter.

We celebrated Ethan's graduation at a restaurant in Rome, then Sophie suggested gelato while everyone argued about visiting Naples next July.

Did you notice that Reebok sponsored the marathon in Nairobi, and that Joseph finished ahead of Grace despite training only since April?

On Friday, my coworker Rebecca booked an Airbnb in Lisbon so she could meet Pedro before flying onward to Casablanca.

I keep telling Charlotte that Verizon offers better coverage than AT&T across Florida, yet she still complains every single Sunday morning.

They watched Taylor perform in Nashville, while Ryan filmed everything on his iPhone and posted it across Instagram before midnight Saturday.

We assured Grandpa that Boeing planes were safe, though William nervously boarded the flight from Frankfurt to Helsinki last cold January.

You really must visit the museum that Claudia recommended in Vienna, especially since Felix said Mozart performed there centuries ago in December.

She drove her Volkswagen from Hamburg to Copenhagen, where Lukas waited with coffee and exciting news about Siemens hiring throughout the autumn.

Have Jonathan and Emily already left for Rio, or are they still waiting for LATAM to confirm their connection through Lima Wednesday?

I told you that David and Maria were driving through Barcelona last August, sipping Starbucks coffee while listening to Coldplay on their brand-new Toyota stereo.
Did you really meet Jennifer at the Google office in Dublin last Friday, or were you actually shopping at Zara near the Liffey River instead?
We spent every Sunday that July exploring Rome with Uncle Robert, eating Nutella crepes and posting photos to Instagram from a tiny café near the Vatican.
Michael promised Jessica that he would book the Hilton in Singapore before December, but Delta canceled his flight from Chicago and ruined the whole surprise.
You should ask Karen whether Amazon delivered the Nike shoes to her apartment in Berlin on Wednesday, because the tracking page still says they're in Munich.
Last September, my cousin Daniel moved from Toronto to Sydney, joined Microsoft as an engineer, and started cheering loudly for the Australian cricket team.
They watched Manchester United beat Chelsea on Saturday at a pub in Liverpool, while James kept refreshing the BBC website for the final score.
I can't believe Emma drove her Honda all the way from Madrid to Lisbon last Tuesday just to buy concert tickets for Beyoncé.
Whenever we visit Amsterdam in spring, Peter insists on renting bikes from a shop near the Rijksmuseum and grabbing lunch at McDonald's afterward.
She told me that Oliver and Sophie celebrated New Year in Edinburgh, drinking Coca-Cola and dancing until dawn while fireworks lit up the castle.
You won't guess who I saw at the Apple store in San Francisco on Thursday — it was Rachel, fresh off her Lufthansa flight from Frankfurt!
My grandparents always traveled to Vienna every October, staying at a quiet hotel near the opera and mailing postcards to relatives back in Boston.
Tom and Lucy argued about whether Netflix or Disney was better while waiting for their Uber outside the stadium in Manchester last night.
We flew Qatar Airways from Doha to Nairobi in June, and Hannah immediately texted her brother Aaron to brag about the amazing view.
Could you remind Steven that the FedEx package from New York must reach the Adidas warehouse in Hamburg before Monday, or we'll lose the contract?
After lunch on Saturday, Grace and her father walked along the Seine, took selfies near the Louvre, and bought macarons from a tiny Parisian bakery.
I really think Brian should email Linda at IBM tomorrow, because the Boston conference begins in April and nobody has confirmed the Marriott reservations yet.
They were thrilled when Ferrari unveiled the new model in Milan, and Marco immediately called his friend Giulia to share the exciting news.
You and Megan should visit Prague next November, ride the tram past the old square, and try the goulash that Henry recommended last year.
My mother packed sandwiches every Friday so that Caleb and I could eat lunch beside Lake Geneva before our Swiss train left for Zurich.
He swore that Nokia phones were unbreakable, but Olivia dropped hers on the platform at King's Cross while rushing to catch the train to Leeds.
We celebrated Thanksgiving in Denver with Aunt Patricia, watching the Broncos lose on Sunday and ordering pizza from Domino's because the turkey burned badly.
Did Anna mention that she and Lucas are flying Ryanair to Athens in May to attend Nathan's wedding near the ancient Acropolis?
I gently reminded Charlotte that Tesla deliveries in Oslo usually slow down each January, so she shouldn't expect her car before the Norwegian holidays end.
You really must tell George how Samantha danced all night at the Hard Rock Café in Las Vegas while Frank lost money at the casino.
Every Wednesday, Isabella practices violin with Mr. Garcia near Central Park, then grabs a bagel and rushes downtown to her internship at Goldman Sachs.
They drove the rented Volkswagen from Vienna to Budapest last spring, blasting ABBA songs and stopping at every Shell station along the Danube highway.
I wonder whether Jack ever finished reading that Stephen King novel he bought in Seattle, because he kept mentioning it during our Skype calls.
When Victoria landed in Cape Town on Monday, she texted Ethan immediately, then ordered a Pepsi and waited patiently for her Avis rental car.
We always meet at the Costa Coffee on Oxford Street, where Natalie and Ryan debate politics until the staff politely ask us to leave.
She insisted that Adidas sponsored the marathon in Boston, but Kevin swore he saw Nike banners everywhere when he ran past Fenway Park.
You should congratulate Patrick because he convinced Spotify to hire him in Stockholm, and now he moves there with Julia right after Christmas.
Last December, the whole family gathered in Munich, drank hot chocolate at the market, and listened to Grandpa Wilhelm tell stories about old Bavaria.
I promise you that Sophie and Marcus will meet us at the Heathrow terminal on Thursday before our British Airways flight departs for Cairo.
They couldn't decide whether to watch the Lakers or the Warriors, so Andrew flipped a coin while Diana ordered tacos from a Los Angeles food truck.
My neighbor Felix swears that he once shook hands with Bill Gates at a Seattle conference, though nobody in our Portland book club believes him.
Whenever you fly Air France into Lyon, remember that Claudia prefers meeting near the Rhône, not at the noisy airport where Thomas always gets lost.
We spent that rainy Tuesday in Dublin listening to U2, eating fish and chips, and arguing with Conor about which Guinness pub was best.
She told Harry that Ford had recalled thousands of trucks across Texas, so he nervously checked his Houston dealership receipt that very afternoon.
I really hope Emily remembers to bring the Canon camera to Venice in April, because Leonardo promised to show us the quietest canals at dawn.
You and Jacob should grab dinner with Chloe in Brussels next Friday, then catch the Eurostar back to London before the late-night fog rolls in.
They were furious when British Airways delayed the flight from Glasgow, so Ben rebooked everyone on KLM and texted Mia the updated Amsterdam schedule.
Grandmother always baked apple pie on Sundays, and little Sam would help her while Grandpa watched golf and cheered for Tiger Woods on television.
I overheard Laura telling Mark that Adobe was opening a new studio in Austin, and she planned to apply before the March deadline closed.
We watched the sunrise over Santorini last July, sipping orange juice from a Greek café while Nina photographed everything for her travel blog.
Did you know that Christopher met Angela at a Toyota dealership in Detroit, and now they vacation together in Mexico every single February?
My brother Joseph keeps insisting that Pepsi tastes better than Coke, but Hannah and I prefer the lemonade they serve at Wimbledon each summer.
You really shouldn't have told Samuel about the Disney trip, because now Olivia expects all of us to fly to Orlando next April together.
They quietly celebrated Diwali in Mumbai with Priya's family, lighting lamps on the balcony and sharing sweets that Grandmother had prepared since Wednesday.
